versions.description: A turn-based strategy game made during my Artificial Intelligence in Games module at NYP (Game Development & Technology). The project was mainly for learning pathfinding and finite state machines, and was built in C++ using a custom engine provided by the school.
versions.title: AI Pathfinding Game
versions.vignette: A turn-based game demonstrating AI pathfinding algorithms.
what_i_did: Implemented A* and BFS pathfinding for AI unit navigation.
Built a finite state machine to manage AI behavior states.
Added a fog-of-war vision system to limit player visibility.
Created a procedural maze generator for randomized maps.
Integrated IrrKlang for sound effects and audio feedback.

versions.description: A Geometry Wars-inspired top-down shooter built solo in Unity (C#) as my final year project at NYP. Players accumulate points by defeating enemies across two modes: Classic (wave-based with boss waves every fourth wave) and Onslaught (endless spawning with increasing intensity and no bosses). All gameplay code, UI, audio integration, and systems were built from scratch over 16 weeks.
versions.title: Electronica
versions.vignette: A Geometry Wars-inspired top-down shooter built solo in Unity.
what_i_did: Built the full game solo in Unity (C#) over 16 weeks, including all gameplay, UI, audio, and systems.
Implemented five enemy types with distinct behaviors: melee, weirdo (elliptical movement), ranged, charger (boost with damage vulnerability window), and dodger (evades incoming shots).

Designed and programmed four boss fights: Devouring Maw (rotating multi-directional shooting), Ascendant Maw (scaling laser count as health drops), Super Charger (variable charge speed), and The Wall (lateral movement, weak point only).
Built a three-pool object pooling system (UI, general, enemy) with configurable expansion limits to manage all spawned objects.
Implemented two separate local leaderboards (Classic and Onslaught), each storing up to 8 score-sorted entries.

versions.title: The Last Survivor
versions.vignette: A 2D top-down survival shooter with wave-based enemy spawning, AI pathfinding, and a multi-phase boss, built in Unity.
versions.description: The Last Survivor is a 2D top-down survival shooter developed as a team project at NYP, targeting PC and Android. Players defend against endless waves of increasingly difficult enemies using weapons, turrets, and a shop system. My work covered all enemy and AI systems — spawning, pathfinding, FSM-driven behavior for three enemy types, friendly turret AI, a multi-phase boss fight, and supporting VFX and sound effects.
what_i_did: Built the enemy spawning system and round progression, with enemies scaling in strength each wave and dedicated boss rounds.
Implemented AI pathfinding and finite state machines for three enemy types: melee zombie, ranged servitor, and a multi-phase boss.
Programmed shielded enemy variants with invulnerability until shield nodes are destroyed, and attack nodes that shoot at the player.
Built friendly turret AI with three turret tiers that automatically target and shoot enemies when activated.
Added sound effects for enemy and combat events, VFX, and the game over screen.

versions.description: A VR horror puzzle game built for the HTC Vive in Unity. This was a final-year project where I worked with art students, continuing from a prototype started by another student group in the previous semester. The game focuses on motion-based interactions, environmental puzzles, and scripted horror events in a first-person VR setting.
versions.title: L.O.C.U.S
versions.vignette: A virtual reality horror puzzle game for the HTC Vive.
what_i_did: Took over an existing project codebase and continued development from a previous trimester team.
Implemented HTC Vive controller input and interaction logic in Unity (C#).
Built and refined core gameplay systems including puzzles, scripted events, and progression flow.
Integrated gameplay with art assets and level content produced by the art team.

versions.description: A Peggle-inspired physics game made during my Physics for Game Programing module. The project was built in C++ using a custom school-provided engine, mwith a focus on applying basic physics concepts to gameplay.
versions.title: Simple Physics Game
versions.vignette: A Peggle-inspired physics game built in C++.
what_i_did: Implemented basic physics simulation (gravity and impulse forces) for moving objects.
Built custom collision detection and response using vector math.
Integrated IrrKlang for sound effects and audio playback.

versions.title: Iron Age
versions.vignette: A first-person medieval RPG with inventory, shops, quests, and AI-driven enemies, built in C++.
versions.description: Iron Age is a team-built first-person medieval RPG developed during my time at NYP. Players explore a 3D open world, complete quests, fight enemies, and manage gear through a shop and inventory system. My work covered the full item and inventory system, shop logic, sound, UI screens, interaction/collision checks, and in-game minigame animations.
what_i_did: Built the full inventory system using a linked list (CInventory, CItemNode, CBaseItem) with support for weapons, armour, and miscellaneous items.
Implemented the shop system — item browsing, buying (clones item into player inventory), and selling (removes item, reimburses gold).
Implemented the sound system using IrrKlang for background music and sound effects tied to game events.
Built UI screens for inventory, shop, and pause, plus camera movement logic on the main menu.
Implemented interaction and collision checks for player-world objects.
Created minigame animations for forging, tree-felling, crafting, and a rotating item preview.

versions.title: Simple Shooter
versions.vignette: A 3D open-world FPS with timed challenges, enemy AI, pickups, and a full objective system, built in C++.
versions.description: A 3D open-world first-person shooter developed as a team project at NYP. Players complete a series of challenges across multiple zones in the shortest time possible. My work covered a large portion of the game's core systems: AI, pickups, weapons, objectives, audio, UI, weather, and open-world loading.
what_i_did: Implemented enemy AI and collision boxes, with guaranteed pickup drops on enemy death.
Built the weapon system including a pulse rifle, shotgun, auto rifle, weapon recoil, and bullet spray.
Designed a base objective class with elimination and bounty objective types, plus an objective indicator.
Implemented the full audio system using irrKlang: looping music playlist, 3D positional sound, and 2D sound.
Built the minimap to display enemies and world objects.
Created the UI manager and all UI screens: main menu, pause, options, and win screen.
Implemented a timed challenge mode, randomised weather (rain/snow), and open-world zone loading.
Built the leaderboard and save/load system for player data.

versions.title: LOST
versions.vignette: A maze RPG with hidden exploration, random encounters, and a Rock Paper Scissors combat system, built in C++.
versions.description: LOST is a team-built maze game with RPG elements, developed during my time at NYP. The game features 6 levels, a hidden fog-of-war map, random encounters, and a Rock Paper Scissors combat system. My work covered all UI screens and their logic, battle system updates, and cosmetic customization features.
what_i_did: Built UI for all major screens: main menu, shop, battle, map, and options.
Implemented logic for all menus including shop interactions and purchase flow.
Updated and extended the battle system logic.
Added background clouds and logic to allow players to change character and map colours.

versions.description: An anime image upscaler built using ESRGAN and fine-tuned for anime-style images at 256x256 resolution. The model was trained on a dataset of 15,000+ images to reduce common upscaling artifacts and improve detail when enlarging low-resolution anime artwork. The project also includes a simple Streamlit interface for running upscales interactively.
versions.title: Anime Image Upscaler
versions.vignette: An ESRGAN-based image upscaler fine-tuned for anime-style images.
what_i_did: Prepared and curated a 15,000+ image dataset for anime-style ESRGAN fine-tuning.
Ran ESRGAN fine-tuning experiments and adjusted training settings to improve output quality.
Built a Streamlit UI to upload images and preview upscaled results.

versions.description: A Raspberry Pi Zero 2W project that detects birds and deters them using a laser pointer, combining camera input, audio triggers, servos, and a simple control state machine. It supports both on-device and remote detection by sending data over MQTT. My role focused on system integration, hardware interfacing, and overall architecture, while teammates handled model training and the YOLO implementation
versions.title: Bird Laser Targeter on the Edge
versions.vignette: A Raspberry Pi Zero 2W-based bird detection and deterrence system.
what_i_did: Integrated the Raspberry Pi hardware stack (camera, servos, laser diode, and audio trigger input).
Built the control flow as a state machine to coordinate detection and targeting behavior.
Implemented MQTT messaging to support remote/offloaded detection and device-server communication.
Assembled the end-to-end system so detection, targeting, and actuation worked together reliably.

versions.description: A digital card game built in Unity around Japanese language learning. This started as a university design thinking assignment where we were supposed to create a physical board game, but I convinced my group to build a digital game instead since it was faster and cheaper to prototype. I implemented the game from scratch in Unity and handled the programming work.
versions.title: Japanese Learning Card Game
versions.vignette: A digital card game built in Unity around Japanese language learning.
what_i_did: Implemented the full game in Unity (C#) completely on my own.
Built an object-oriented card system to define card data, effects, and behaviors.
Implemented turn flow, deck/hand management, and win/loss conditions.

versions.description: A real-time resource management simulation built in Java using LibGDX. This project focused on implementing a small game loop with structured game objects, basic UI, and scene management for switching between gameplay and menu screens.
versions.title: Resource Management Game
versions.vignette: A real-time resource management simulation built in Java using LibGDX.
what_i_did: Designed object-oriented game object classes in Java to support core game mechanics.
Implemented scene flow (menus and gameplay) including transitions and UI rendering in LibGDX.

versions.description: Developed a fully-integrated semi-autonomous robotic car powered by Raspberry Pi Pico with WiFi connectivity. The system combines remote control via gesture-based accelerometer input with autonomous features including line-following using IR sensors, barcode decoding, obstacle detection via ultrasonic sensors, and real-time speed/distance tracking through wheel encoders. The project emphasizes resource-constrained embedded systems design, requiring efficient firmware implementation and multi-component sensor integration.
versions.vignette: A WiFi-controlled robotic car with autonomous obstacle detection and line-following capabilities using embedded sensors and real-time control algorithms.
what_i_did: Implemented IR-based wheel encoder drivers with GPIO interrupts for real-time speed calculation and distance tracking on both wheels, providing feedback for PID motor control.
Developed HC-SR04+ ultrasonic sensor integration with echo timing measurement, timeout handling, and Kalman filtering for accurate obstacle detection and collision avoidance.
Designed and implemented a 6-state finite state machine managing transitions between WiFi connection, remote control, and autonomous navigation with coordinated sensor polling and safety constraints.
Integrated dual-core architecture with Core 0 handling real-time control logic and Core 1 managing telemetry transmission to dashboard.
versions.title: Embedded Car Project

versions.description:
Built with Flutter (Web) and deployed with Docker on Google Cloud, this is my personal portfolio site for hosting and organizing my work online.

Since version 1.2, I made major usability and layout changes. The previous structure made it hard to browse all projects without excessive scrolling, so I introduced horizontal carousels to improve discoverability and scanning speed. While implementing that, I also refactored the overall page design into a more compact layout, replacing the heavier card-first presentation used across earlier versions.
versions.title: Personal Portfolio Website
versions.vignette: A portfolio website built on Flutter.
what_i_did: Implemented horizontal project carousels to reduce scrolling and improve browsing across project groups.
Added a dedicated project summary page with grouped sections for browsing by project categories.
Refactored the site layout into a more compact structure across landing, summary, projects, and detail pages.
Preserved Docker-based deployment on Google Cloud.

versions.description:
Built with Flutter (Web) and deployed with Docker on Google Cloud, this is my personal portfolio site for hosting and organizing my work online.

Version 1.2 focused on restructuring the data model and improving the landing page. I updated how page data is stored so that projects now define which pages they belong to, instead of pages defining their projects which reduced the amount of cross-file editing needed when adding or updating projects. I also expanded the landing page with a personal history timeline that links to all my projects in chronological order, making it easier to see my work and education history at a glance.
versions.title: Personal Portfolio Website
versions.vignette: A portfolio website built on Flutter.
what_i_did: Refactored project and page data flow so projects define their own page membership.
Implemented a year-based timeline section on the landing page to show project, work, and education history.
Reworked project list and detail layouts into a denser structure.
Maintained Docker-based deployment on Google Cloud.

versions.description:
Built with Flutter (Web) and deployed with Docker on Google Cloud, this is my personal portfolio site for hosting and organizing my work online.

After getting the first Flutter version working end-to-end, I was happy with the layout but found the page felt too stale visually. Version 1.1 focused on adding more color throughout the site to make it more engaging. I updated the project cards with richer styling and expanded the color palette across the interface. I also restructured the project data model to support multiple versions per project, so the site can show how a project evolved over time.
versions.title: Personal Portfolio Website
versions.vignette: A portfolio website built on Flutter.
what_i_did: Upgraded the Flutter site styling with a stronger color system and richer card presentation.
Implemented multi-version project entries so each project can show version history in one detail view.
Improved projects-page controls with filtering, search, sorting, and list/grid browsing behavior.
Maintained Docker-based deployment on Google Cloud.

versions.description:
Built with Flutter (Web) and deployed with Docker on Google Cloud, this is my personal portfolio site for hosting and organizing my work online. I moved from a Streamlit version to Flutter because Streamlit felt limiting for responsive layouts, and Flutter gave me better control over how the site adapts across desktop and mobile screens. Since I had Flutter experience from work, I migrated the site by adapting the existing JSON and image assets to work with Flutter's rendering system.

Project content is stored in structured JSON files and rendered into consistent project entries at runtime, so updates are mainly done by editing metadata. The goal was a serviceable, easy-to-update portfolio that presents my work clearly.
versions.title: Personal Portfolio Website
versions.vignette: A portfolio website built on Flutter.
what_i_did: Built a Flutter Web portfolio with responsive layouts for desktop and mobile.
Structured project and page content as JSON for data-driven rendering.
Packaged the site with Docker for consistent deployment.
Hosted the deployed site on Google Cloud.

versions.description: A personal portfolio site built with Python + Streamlit to get a simple web presence up quickly without needing much frontend work. The site's content (projects and pages) is stored in a JSON file, and each page is generated from that data so I can update the portfolio by editing entries rather than rewriting the UI. It's deployed on Google Cloud in a Docker container.
versions.title: Personal Portfolio Website
versions.vignette: A portfolio website built with Streamlit and Python.
what_i_did: Built the portfolio site in Streamlit with a simple multi-page layout.
Implemented tag and category filtering for browsing projects.
Set up Docker-based deployment on Google Cloud for hosting and updates.
Implemented a ProjectObject-based renderer to generate pages from structured project metadata.

versions.description: This version of Spending Dashboard is a full frontend-backend rebuild of my earlier PySide6 desktop app. I moved away from PySide6 because the app felt too slow to launch and navigate as the project grew. The new architecture uses a Vue.js frontend for the UI and a FastAPI backend for data processing and API endpoints, packaged in Docker so I can run it locally in a consistent environment. The result is a faster, more responsive workflow for loading, filtering, and visualizing spending data while keeping deployment simple on my own PC.
versions.title: Spending Dashboard
versions.vignette: A Dockerized personal spending dashboard rebuilt with a Vue.js frontend and FastAPI backend for faster startup and smoother data exploration.
what_i_did: Built FastAPI endpoints for loading and serving spending data to the UI.
Implemented the Vue.js dashboard interface for tables, filters, and spending visualizations.
Containerized the application with Docker so the full stack runs locally with consistent setup and faster startup.

versions.description: A PySide6 (Qt) desktop application for tracking, analyzing, and visualizing personal spending data. I moved from a Streamlit dashboard to PySide6 because I wanted to build the same kind of tool without a web-based frontend. The app loads purchase records from a structured .xlsx file, supports filtering and plotting, and includes utilities for maintaining and backing up the data. It includes a dashboard view, a sortable table view, and Excel file automation powered by openpyxl in an offline desktop workflow.
versions.title: Spending Dashboard
versions.vignette: A PySide6 desktop application for tracking and analyzing personal spending data.
what_i_did: Built a multi-page PySide6 desktop app (dashboard + table view).
Automated Excel validation, formatting, and backups with openpyxl.
Added interactive charts for trends and category breakdowns.

versions.description: A Streamlit dashboard for tracking, visualizing, and analyzing personal spending data. It reads purchase records from a structured Excel (.xlsx) file, supports interactive filtering and search, and presents trends through charts and summary views. The project also includes an Excel auto-formatting pipeline to keep data entry consistent and easier to maintain over time.
versions.title: Spending Dashboard
versions.vignette: A Streamlit app for tracking and analyzing personal spending data.
what_i_did: Built a Streamlit dashboard for browsing and searching spending records.
Added interactive filters for date, cost, and category.
Created Plotly charts for trends and breakdowns.
Automated Excel formatting and conditional styling with openpyxl.

versions.description: A self-hosted full-stack web application for in-depth YouTube channel analytics. Syncs data from the YouTube Data API v3 and Analytics API v2 into a local SQLite database via an incremental sync engine designed to minimise API quota usage. Presents the data through a custom interactive dashboard with hand-built SVG charts, spike detection, traffic source breakdowns, and AI-powered comment analysis. Includes a thumbnail A/B testing tool that previews uploaded thumbnails inside simulated YouTube layouts (Home feed, Search results, Video Player sidebar) populated with real competitor videos matched by a fine-tuned sentence-transformer embedding model.
versions.title: YouTube Analytics Dashboard
versions.vignette: A self-hosted YouTube analytics dashboard with AI-powered thumbnail testing and comment analysis.
what_i_did: Built an analytics dashboard with time-series charts, spike detection, and traffic source breakdowns for YouTube channel data.
Created a thumbnail testing tool to preview uploaded thumbnails in simulated YouTube Home, Search, and Video Player layouts.
Fine-tuned a sentence-transformer model to find contextually similar competitor videos by title for the thumbnail testing feature.
Added AI-powered comment summarization and a word cloud generator with configurable LLM support.
Designed an incremental sync system that pulls channel data and analytics from the YouTube API with quota estimation and progress tracking.
Built per-video and per-playlist analytics pages with breakdown tabs for metrics, engagement, monetization, and discovery.

versions.description:
A tool I built to help with fan translations for Japanese drama CDs and games. It handles transcription, context-building, and subtitle translation in one workflow, so I can generate a first pass and then review and edit the results.

Since v1.1, I refactored both transcription and LLM usage behind backend interfaces, which made it much easier to add or swap providers (for example local models and API-based models with different keys/configs). I also redesigned parts of the UI based on real day-to-day usage to improve flow and reduce friction, and removed lower-value features like Tavily search to keep the app focused on core translation work.

download_paths.key: Gakumas Lilja Main Story STEP3 English Translation
Gakumas Lilja Main Story STEP2 English Translation

versions.title: Translator Helper
versions.vignette: A full-stack assistant for transcribing, and translating subtitle files with Angular and FastAPI.
what_i_did: Built an Angular frontend with a FastAPI backend to manage transcription, context, and translation in one place.
Used Whisper to transcribe short voice snippets for individual lines when needed.
Generated translation context by parsing .srt/.ass files and pulling supporting web info.
Produced first-draft translations for entire subtitle files to speed up review and editing.

versions.description:
A tool I built to help with fan translations for Japanese drama CDs and games. It handles transcription, context-building, and subtitle translation in one workflow, so I can generate a first pass and then review and edit the results.

I originally built this in Streamlit, but I found it wasn't very responsive, so I remade it with a proper frontend-backend split using Angular and FastAPI (Python) to make the app feel snappier.

download_paths.key: Gakumas Lilja Main Story STEP1 English Translation

versions.title: Translator Helper
versions.vignette: A full-stack assistant for transcribing, and translating subtitle files with Angular and FastAPI.
what_i_did: Built an Angular frontend with a FastAPI backend to manage transcription, context, and translation in one place.
Used Whisper to transcribe short voice snippets for individual lines when needed.
Generated translation context by parsing .srt/.ass files and pulling supporting web info.
Produced first-draft translations for entire subtitle files to speed up review and editing.

versions.description: An application that speeds up the process of transcribing and translating anime drama CDs using OpenAI’s API and Whisper. It can generate a first-draft transcription and translation for an entire audio file, export the results into subtitle formats for review, and provide smaller tools for handling individual lines or short audio snippets when needed.
versions.title: Translator Helper
versions.vignette: An AI-powered tool to assist with transcribing and translating anime drama CDs using LLMs and Whisper.
what_i_did: Generated first-draft translations for entire subtitle files to speed up review and editing.
Used Whisper to transcribe full audio files and short snippets when lines were unclear.
Collected translation context by parsing .srt/.ass files and pulling supporting web info.
Provided a grading view to evaluate translations for accuracy, fluency, and cultural fit.

versions.description: A personal tool for helping with transcribing and translating anime drama CDs using LLMs and Whisper. I made it because there are times when I can’t clearly catch a line or I’m unsure how to phrase a translation, so the app helps by generating transcripts, organizing context, and producing translation outputs in a few styles to compare.
versions.title: Translator Helper
versions.vignette: An AI-powered tool to assist with transcribing and translating anime drama CDs using LLMs and Whisper.
what_i_did: Built a translation view that outputs natural, literal, and annotated versions.
Added transcription from audio files or microphone input using Whisper.
Added context support by parsing subtitle files and pulling supporting web info.
Built a grading view to review translations for fluency, accuracy, and cultural fit.

versions.description: A Python project that pulls video and comment data from my YouTube channel using the YouTube Data API, then runs keyword and sentiment analysis to see what topics show up most often. It generates separate word clouds for positive and negative comments to visualize common terms, mainly as a small experiment in sentiment analysis and basic text visualization.
versions.title: YouTube Comment Keyword Search
versions.vignette: A Python project that analyzes YouTube comments for keyword and sentiment trends.
what_i_did: Pulled video and comment data from the YouTube Data API and cleaned it for analysis.
Implemented sentiment scoring over comments to separate positive vs negative text.
Generated word clouds and simple plots to visualize common keywords by sentiment.

versions.description: A Python tool that automates the yearly setup of my budgeting spreadsheets by generating new sheets, inserting formulas, and applying formatting automatically. It keeps calculations consistent and improves readability with conditional formatting. This was later replaced by my Spending Dashboard project.
versions.title: Budgeting Spreadsheet Automation
versions.vignette: A Python tool to automate the yearly setup of budgeting spreadsheets.
what_i_did: Automated yearly sheet creation, formula insertion, and formatting in Excel with Python.
Added conditional formatting rules to keep categories and totals readable.
Built a small Tkinter GUI to run the setup without editing code.

versions.description:
A full remake of my original Diamond City Radio player, rebuilt in Flutter to make it actually usable day to day on phone and desktop. The first Java/LibGDX version proved the concept, but the UI quality was poor and background playback on mobile was not reliable enough for normal use.

In this version, I redesigned the app with a Pip-Boy-inspired interface, responsive desktop/mobile layouts, and a cleaner playback flow that rotates intros, songs, outros, and DJ reports. I also added persistent visual/audio settings (display color, scanlines, scanline width/distance/speed, and volume controls) and improved the overall architecture for queue/state management.

Note: audio files are not included in the public repository.
versions.title: Diamond City Radio (Fallout-Inspired Music Player)
versions.vignette: A Fallout-inspired radio player that mixes songs and DJ reports with a Pip-Boy-style interface.
what_i_did: Remade the original Java/LibGDX app in Flutter to fix UI quality and mobile background playback limitations.
Built a responsive Pip-Boy-style interface for both desktop and phone layouts.
Implemented radio-style set playback that interleaves intros, songs, outros, and DJ reports.
Added persistent visual and audio settings, including scanline controls and independent SFX/hum/main volume settings.

versions.description: A Java-based music player inspired by Fallout 4's Diamond City Radio. It plays a mix of music tracks and DJ voice clips, inserting commentary between songs to recreate the feel of an in-game radio station.
versions.title: Diamond City Radio (Fallout-Inspired Music Player)
versions.vignette: A Java-based music player inspired by Fallout 4's Diamond City Radio.
what_i_did: Built the app in LibGDX (Java), including the UI and playback controls.
Implemented audio loading and management for music tracks and voice clips.
Created the radio-style playback flow that interleaves DJ commentary between songs.
